title: GDF-11
description: Growth differentiation factor 11 (GDF-11) is a protein in the TGF-β superfamily that acts as a negative regulator of erythropoiesis and plays a critical role in embryonic development and tissue homeostasis. It functions as a ligand for activin type II receptors, and while historically debated as a 'rejuvenation factor,' current research focuses on its role in inhibiting red blood cell maturation and its association with aging-related physiological changes.

research.title: Activin Receptor II Ligand Traps: New Treatment Paradigm for Low-Risk MDS
research.summary: This review identifies GDF-11 as a negative regulator of terminal erythroid differentiation and explores how trapping this ligand can treat anemia in myelodysplastic syndromes.

research.title: Elritercept, a modified activin receptor IIA ligand trap, increased erythropoiesis and thrombopoiesis in a phase 1 trial
research.summary: Clinical study showing that inhibiting GDF-11 and related ligands can safely increase the production of red blood cells and platelets in humans.

research.title: GDF11 is increased in patients with aplastic anemia
research.summary: Research findings show that high levels of GDF-11 are significantly correlated with impaired red blood cell production in patients with aplastic anemia.

research.title: Lifelong exercise, but not short-term high-intensity interval training, increases GDF11, a marker of successful aging
research.summary: Preliminary evidence suggesting that GDF-11 levels are higher in older men who have maintained lifelong exercise habits compared to sedentary peers.

research.title: Age Trends in Growth and Differentiation Factor-11 and Myostatin Levels in Healthy Men
research.summary: A study using advanced mass spectrometry to clarify the relationship between GDF-11, aging, and muscle growth regulators in healthy males.

sentiment.summary: Scientific consensus is split; while research suggests GDF-11 inhibition treats blood disorders, early 'anti-aging' claims are heavily debated, and user community interest centers on its role as a biomarker rather than a direct supplement.

effects.label: Blood Disorders:
effects.description: Highly effective target for treating MDS-related anemia via ligand trap drugs like Luspatercept.

effects.label: Rejuvenation Debate:
effects.description: Evidence for its role as a 'fountain of youth' remains controversial with conflicting results in animal and human trials.

effects.label: Exercise Synergy:
effects.description: Shows long-term sensitivity to exercise habits rather than short-term high-intensity training.

effects.label: Cancer Prognostic:
effects.description: High levels are unfortunately associated with shorter survival in specific cancers like uveal melanoma.

dosage.label: Clinical Inhibition:
dosage.description: In trials, GDF-11 inhibitors are dosed between 0.05 to 4.5 mg/kg for therapeutic effect.

dosage.label: Natural Promotion:
dosage.description: No direct supplemental dose exists; lifelong resistance training is the primary documented way to influence levels.

dosage.label: Monitoring:
dosage.description: Blood assessment via LC-MS/MS is required to accurately determine endogenous levels.

sideEffects.label: Anemia Risk:
sideEffects.description: Excessive natural levels or over-expression can lead to suppressed red blood cell counts.

sideEffects.label: Oncogenic Potential:
sideEffects.description: Upregulation is linked to poor outcomes in certain malignant conditions.

sideEffects.label: Erythrocytosis:
sideEffects.description: In therapeutic contexts, blocking GDF-11 can lead to abnormally high red blood cell or platelet counts.

availability.label: Prescription Only:
availability.description: Therapeutic agents targeting GDF-11 (like Luspatercept) are strictly prescription-only for blood disorders.

availability.label: Peptide Markets:
availability.description: Often found in research-only peptide stores, though human data for direct GDF-11 peptide self-administration is largely lacking.
